2021-07-01.title: A poll reveals another U.S. vaccine divide, this one by household.
2021-07-01.abstract: Workers were more likely to get a shot when their employers encouraged them and provided paid time off.

2021-07-01.title: Pictures From a Revolution: China’s Communists Mark a Centennial
2021-07-01.abstract: The improbable rise of a superpower that was born in the rubble of dynasty and fears it may yet meet the same fate.

2021-07-01.title: Israel’s New Leaders Tilt Right With Settler Deal, Hinting at Approach
2021-07-01.abstract: Prime Minister Naftali Bennett’s decision about an unauthorized settlement has provided an early idea of how he hopes to manage his unlikely coalition.

2021-07-01.title: Digital vaccination cards go into effect in Europe, with some turbulence.
2021-07-01.abstract: “This can only work for travelers if member states implement it in a harmonized way,” said an official representing Europe’s largest air carriers.

2021-07-01.title: Marking Party’s Centennial, Xi Warns That China Will Not Be Bullied
2021-07-01.abstract: A century after the Communist Party’s founding, China’s leader said foreign powers would “crack their heads and spill blood” if they tried to stop its rise.

2021-07-02.title: The Best Movies and TV Shows New to Netflix, Amazon and Stan in Australia in July
2021-07-02.abstract: Our streaming picks for July, including ‘Fear Street,’ ‘The Comeback Trail’ and ‘The Tomorrow War’

2021-07-02.title: Labour Holds On to U.K. Parliament Seat, Easing Pressure on Its Leader
2021-07-02.abstract: The election this week of the sister of Jo Cox, a lawmaker who was killed in 2016, was seen as a victory for Labour’s leader in a region where Prime Minister Boris Johnson’s Conservatives had made big inroads.

2021-07-02.title: Australia’s Path Out of the Pandemic: Will It Make You Scream With Anger or Joy?
2021-07-02.abstract: The prime minister has finally acknowledged that Australia will eventually have to learn to live with Covid.

2021-07-02.title: U.S. Leaves Its Last Afghan Base, Effectively Ending Operations
2021-07-02.abstract: With little fanfare, Bagram Air Base — once the military’s nerve center — was handed over to the Afghans, after nearly 20 years of waging war from the hub.

2021-07-02.title: On the Scene: A Turnabout in the Tigray War
2021-07-02.abstract: We were in northern Ethiopia this week when residents poured into the streets of Mekelle, the capital of the Tigray region, to celebrate an unexpected military victory.

2021-07-03.title: Landslide in Japanese Resort Town Leaves at Least 19 Missing
2021-07-03.abstract: A torrent of mud and debris washed away homes in Atami, about 60 miles southwest of Tokyo, after more than a foot of rain fell in 48 hours.

2021-07-03.title: Journalist’s Monthslong Hunger Strike Points to Perils of Reporting in Morocco
2021-07-03.abstract: Soulaimane Raissouni, who has been held without trial for over a year, is more than 80 days into a hunger strike.

2021-07-03.title: These Chinese Millennials Are ‘Chilling,’ and Beijing Isn’t Happy
2021-07-03.abstract: Young people in China have set off a nascent counterculture movement that involves lying down and doing as little as possible.

2021-07-03.title: The Relics of America’s War in Afghanistan
2021-07-03.abstract: Just a mile from Bagram Air Base, where U.S. forces departed on Thursday, shops sell items left over from two decades of fighting. Each one tells a story.

2021-07-03.title: Danger Awaited in Myanmar. So He Made a Daring Bid to Stay in Japan.
2021-07-03.abstract: After defying Myanmar’s military rulers at a soccer match, Ko Pyae Lyan Aung decided to seek asylum. But he was being watched.

2021-07-04.title: ‘Red Cradles’ Nurture China’s Next Generation of Communist Leaders
2021-07-04.abstract: Communist Party schools and academies are inculcating Chinese officials in Xi Jinping Thought as the country shifts in a more authoritarian direction.

2021-07-04.title: Indian police investigate whether scammers gave thousands of shots of salt water instead of vaccine.
2021-07-04.abstract: Fourteen people have been charged with conspiracy and forgery, the latest example of fraud undermining India’s pandemic response.

2021-07-04.title: Philippine Military Plane Crashes With 96 People Aboard
2021-07-04.abstract: At least 50 people, including three civilians on the ground, were killed in the accident, and it was feared that the toll would climb.

2021-07-04.title: A Diamond Rush in South Africa, Born of Desperation and Distrust
2021-07-04.abstract: When rumors of a diamond find hit social media, thousands of jobless South Africans rushed to a sleepy village. The government’s claim that the discovery was actually quartz was met with suspicion.

2021-07-04.title: As Secular Peace Effort Stutters in Israel, Religious Mediators Hope to Step In
2021-07-04.abstract: The inclusion of an Islamist party in Israel’s government has spurred a group of imams and rabbis hoping to build a religious-based peace movement.

2021-07-05.title: Japanese Resort Town Searching for 80 People After Mudslide
2021-07-05.abstract: Four victims have been declared dead, and the authorities are trying to determine whether the dozens of missing were caught up in a disaster set off by record rains.

2021-07-05.title: They Were the Nice, Older Couple Next Door. Then the First Body Turned Up.
2021-07-05.abstract: An Iranian couple has confessed to murdering and dismembering their son, years after killing a daughter and her husband. They are not sorry.

2021-07-05.title: Vatican Says Pope Is in ‘Good General Condition’ After Colon Surgery
2021-07-05.abstract: The 84-year-old pontiff was “alert” after a three-hour operation, but he is expected to stay in the hospital in Rome for a week.

2021-07-05.title: Canada’s Grim Legacy of Cultural Erasure, in Poignant School Photos
2021-07-05.abstract: With the discovery of human remains on the grounds of residential schools, this history is now dominating the conversation in the country.

2021-07-05.title: As Lebanon’s Crisis Deepens, Lines for Fuel Grow, and Food and Medicine Are Scarce
2021-07-05.abstract: The World Bank said the financial crisis could rank among the world’s three worst since the mid-1800s. The currency has lost more than 90 percent of its value and unemployment has skyrocketed.

2021-07-06.title: Israel’s New Government Fails to Extend Contentious Citizenship Law
2021-07-06.abstract: In an early setback for Prime Minister Naftali Bennett, parts of his coalition opposed the law. The opposition refused to come to the rescue.

2021-07-06.title: A French Teenager’s Anti-Islam Rant Unleashed Death Threats. Now 13 Are on Trial.
2021-07-06.abstract: The court case is about online harassment, but it also touches on some of France’s most sensitive issues: freedom of expression and blasphemy.

2021-07-06.title: Plane Crashes in Russia With 28 People Aboard
2021-07-06.abstract: The aircraft was flying a regional route in the far eastern peninsula of Kamchatka. There were not believed to be any survivors, Russian news agencies reported.

2021-07-06.title: White flags fly in Malaysia as hunger spreads during lockdown.
2021-07-06.abstract: With a fourth lockdown in place, tens of thousands of Malaysians have lost their jobs. A campaign is expressing dissatisfaction with the government.

2021-07-06.title: U.K. Proposes Moving Asylum Seekers Abroad While Their Cases Are Decided
2021-07-06.abstract: Rights advocates swiftly condemned the new migration plan, which they say not only violates international law but also would be impossible to carry out.

2021-07-07.title: Dilip Kumar, Film Star Who Brought Realism to Bollywood, Dies at 98
2021-07-07.abstract: One of India’s earliest Method actors, he was the last survivor of a triumvirate of actors who ruled Hindi cinema in the 1950s and ’60s.

2021-07-07.title: Critic’s Death Puts Focus on Palestinian Authority’s Authoritarianism
2021-07-07.abstract: Protests against the increasingly autocratic rule of President Mahmoud Abbas and his limits on freedom have been harshly suppressed.

2021-07-07.title: French Court Convicts 11 People of Harassing Teenager Over Anti-Islam Rant
2021-07-07.abstract: In a case that fueled a fierce debate about free speech and blasphemy, the court found 10 people guilty of harassment and one of making death threats.

2021-07-07.title: Over the weekend, New Yorkers re-emerged in a changed city.
2021-07-07.abstract: It was once possible to envisage New York City coming back entirely. Now, whatever lies next for the city feels more like a giant collective improvisation, a city taking shape on the fly. The holiday weekend was a time to rediscover what New York was, and glimpse what it might become.

2021-07-07.title: Haiti says four suspects in assassination have been killed and two arrested.
2021-07-07.abstract: Jovenel Moïse died in an attack on his private residence on the outskirts of the capital, Port-au-Prince. His wife was also shot. The prime minister said a group of attackers was involved.

2021-07-08.title: Former South African President Is Arrested
2021-07-08.abstract: Jacob Zuma, the former president of South Africa, was arrested on Wednesday and will begin serving a 15-month prison sentence after being found guilty of contempt for failing to appear before a commission investigating corruption.

2021-07-08.title: Haiti’s political turmoil may hamper efforts to contain the virus, the W.H.O. says.
2021-07-08.abstract: The pandemic has grown worse in recent weeks in Haiti, which has yet to begin vaccinating its citizens.

2021-07-08.title: Two U.S. citizens are among at least 17 suspects arrested in the Moïse killing.
2021-07-08.abstract: Haitian officials identified the American suspects as James Solages and Joseph Vincent, both of Haitian descent.

2021-07-08.title: Spectators Will Be Barred at Tokyo Olympics Amid New Covid Emergency
2021-07-08.abstract: Organizers announced in June that domestic fans would be allowed, despite concerns that the Games could be a superspreader event. A sudden rise in cases upended those plans.

2021-07-08.title: Why Haiti Still Despairs After $13 Billion in Foreign Aid
2021-07-08.abstract: Since a powerful earthquake devastated the country in 2010, foreign aid seems only to have helped perpetuate some of the country’s biggest troubles.

2021-07-09.title: Chasing After Mysteries in Victoria’s High Country
2021-07-09.abstract: Snow-capped mountains. Lush green valleys. And a string of unsolved disappearances.

2021-07-09.title: Two rivals aim to take control in Haiti as the president’s death leaves a power vacuum.
2021-07-09.abstract: The political storm in Haiti intensified on Thursday as two competing prime ministers claimed the right to run the country, setting up an extraordinary power struggle over who has the legal authority to govern after the brazen assassination of President Jovenel Moïse in his home the day before.

2021-07-09.title: Two arrested Americans claim they were just ‘translators’ in the assassination plot, a Haitian judge says.
2021-07-09.abstract: The two men maintained that the plot to kill President Jovenel Moïse had been planned intensively for a month, a judge involved with the investigation said.

2021-07-09.title: Architect Finds a Sense of Belonging for His Family’s Homeland, and for Himself
2021-07-09.abstract: Omar Degan was born in Italy to Somali parents who fled before the African nation’s civil war. He’s now helping rebuild the country by looking to the past to build for the future.

2021-07-09.title: What we know and don’t know about the assassination.
2021-07-09.abstract: During an attack in the early hours of Wednesday, a group of assassins fatally shot President Jovenel Moïse of Haiti and wounded his wife, Martine Moïse, in their private residence on the outskirts of the capital, Port-au-Prince.

2021-07-10.title: Who Paid for That Mansion? A Senator or the Haitian People?
2021-07-10.abstract: Valued at $3.4 million, a Haitian senator’s Montreal villa has become a potent emblem of the growing gap between Haiti’s impoverished citizens and its wealthy political elite.

2021-07-10.title: ‘Mother, When Will You Come?’: The Covid Orphans of India
2021-07-10.abstract: Thousands of children lost their parents during a calamitous wave of infections. While the government is vowing to help them, many face the risk of neglect and exploitation when the attention fades.

2021-07-10.title: Jehan Sadat, 87, Widow of Egypt’s President and Women’s Advocate, Dies
2021-07-10.abstract: Until her husband was assassinated in 1981, she pushed him to enact measures aimed at improving women’s rights and education.

2021-07-10.title: In Sri Lanka, the Government Looks Increasingly Like a Family Firm
2021-07-10.abstract: Three brothers of President Gotabaya Rajapaksa now hold top posts in his government. Two of his nephews have official positions, too.

2021-07-10.title: Haitian officials called for U.S. military assistance. Some Haitians say, ‘No, No & No.’
2021-07-10.abstract: Intellectuals, activists and others say the country needs to find a solution on its own, and that international intervention has damaged their country.

2021-07-11.title: Soccer Success Is Making England ‘Whole Again’
2021-07-11.abstract: The stunning rise of England’s soccer team is helping define a new vision of Englishness, putting the lie to those who would stir culture wars.

2021-07-11.title: No Rockets, but Anxiety Persists in Southern Israel
2021-07-11.abstract: The 11-day war between Israel and Hamas ended in May, but for Israelis living and working within range of Gaza, the fear of attack is ever present. We spoke with four people affected by rockets and mortars about their decisions to stay on or leave.

2021-07-11.title: A message said to be from the president’s widow is posted to Twitter.
2021-07-11.abstract: An audio recording claimed to be of Martine Moïse, who was wounded in the attack that killed her husband, urged people to carry on his “battle.” But doubts were raised about the clip’s authenticity.

2021-07-11.title: A number of breath-based coronavirus tests are in development around the world.
2021-07-11.abstract: “It’s clear now, I think, that you can detect this disease with a breath test,” said one scientist.

2021-07-11.title: New Zealand, where Covid-19 is dormant, fights another respiratory virus, and other news from around the world.
2021-07-11.abstract: The country’s lockdowns to keep the coronavirus at bay may have helped spur an outbreak of a flulike illness in children.

2021-07-12.title: What were Colombian military vets doing in Haiti?
2021-07-12.abstract: Colombia has one of the best-trained militaries in Latin America, and because of this, Colombian veterans are highly sought after by global security companies. They deploy them to faraway places like Yemen and Iraq, often paying far more than they could expect to earn in Colombia.

2021-07-12.title: South Asian migrant workers are stranded as they wait for vaccines.
2021-07-12.abstract: Thousands across the region have been left in limbo because of sluggish immunization campaigns, red tape and closed borders.

2021-07-12.title: After Defeat, England’s Black Soccer Players Face a Racist Outburst
2021-07-12.abstract: The social media bigotry, directed at three players who missed penalty kicks in the Euro 2020 final, was condemned by Prime Minister Boris Johnson and most everyone else.

2021-07-12.title: After months in limbo, Taiwan finally has a deal to acquire BioNTech shots.
2021-07-12.abstract: The deal for 10 million vaccine doses eases a serious shortage on the self-ruling island, which has accused Beijing of interfering with previous efforts.

2021-07-12.title: Two Jordanians Sentenced to Prison in Plot Against Monarchy
2021-07-12.abstract: A closed court sentenced a former confidant to Jordan’s king and adviser to the Saudi crown prince, along with a cousin of the monarch, to 15 years in prison.

2021-07-13.title: Several Die as Construction Crane Collapses in British Columbia
2021-07-13.abstract: The police in Kelowna said that the accident, which occurred as the crane was being dismantled, caused multiple fatalities at a condo construction site.

2021-07-13.title: Weighing Haiti Intervention, U.S. Again Faces a Torturous Dilemma
2021-07-13.abstract: Time and again, the world has found that propping up failing states is compelling in the short term and potentially disastrous in the long term.

2021-07-13.title: A graphic Covid-19 ad prompts a backlash in Australia.
2021-07-13.abstract: The video appears to target younger people, most of whom are ineligible for shots in a vaccination campaign that has been slowed by shortages.

2021-07-13.title: South Korea’s top baseball league halts its season after 5 players are infected.
2021-07-13.abstract: The suspension is a turnabout from last year, when Korea’s baseball teams continued to play while U.S. pro sports leagues went dark.

2021-07-14.title: Scotland Cautiously Eases Virus Restrictions
2021-07-14.abstract: On Tuesday, Scotland’s First Minister, Nicola Sturgeon, outlined a careful plan to ease coronavirus restrictions, saying that some rules such as face mask mandates will remain in place.

2021-07-14.title: Big Dreams and False Claims: How Colombians Got Embroiled in Haiti Assassination
2021-07-14.abstract: A revealing look at the effort to recruit Colombian military veterans for what was described as a noble nation-building effort in Haiti but ended with 18 of them arrested and three of them dead.

2021-07-14.title: China’s Women Filmmakers Are Embracing Their Stories. Moviegoers Are Loving It.
2021-07-14.abstract: China has many talented female directors, but few have seen runaway box office success. That changed with two recent movies, “Hi, Mom” and “Sister.”

2021-07-14.title: Parents Who Never Stopped Searching Reunite With Son Abducted 24 Years Ago
2021-07-14.abstract: Guo Gangtang’s cross-country, decades-long search for his son inspired a movie. Now, there’s an ending fit for Hollywood.

2021-07-14.title: 12 Are Killed in Pakistan Bus Explosion, Including 9 Chinese
2021-07-14.abstract: At least some of the passengers were engineers traveling to a Chinese-funded hydropower project in the country’s remote northwest.

2021-07-15.title: Suspects in Haitian President’s Killing Met to Plan a Future Without Him
2021-07-15.abstract: Haitian officials accuse the suspects of meeting to plot President Jovenel Moïse’s assassination, but participants say the sessions were intended to plan a government once the president stepped down.

2021-07-15.title: A blame game intensifies over Australia’s sluggish immunization campaign.
2021-07-15.abstract: Only about 9.5 percent of the population has been fully inoculated. Prime Minister Scott Morrison said that “cautious” guidance from the country’s vaccine advisory body had “put us behind.”

2021-07-15.title: Haiti receives its first shipment of vaccines: 500,000 doses from the U.S.
2021-07-15.abstract: The donation comes amid political turmoil after the assassination of President Jovenel Moïse.

2021-07-15.title: Former Official Wanted by Mexico Takes Refuge in Israel
2021-07-15.abstract: Israel has refused to extradite Tomás Zerón, accused of torture and evidence tampering, because of Mexico’s criticism of the country, an Israeli official said.

2021-07-15.title: Millions of children missed out on basic vaccinations during the pandemic.
2021-07-15.abstract: About 23 million young people were not inoculated against diseases like measles and polio last year. The number was the highest since 2009, the W.H.O. and UNICEF said.

2021-07-16.title: Africa’s Covid Crisis Deepens, but Vaccines Are Still Far Off
2021-07-16.abstract: Rich nations have bought up most doses long into the future, and there is little hope of a rescue in sight.

2021-07-16.title: Ugandan Weight Lifter Missing in Japan Amid Olympic Lockdown
2021-07-16.abstract: Julius Ssekitoleko, 20, an Olympic hopeful, failed to show up Friday for a required coronavirus test. Games organizers have ordered all participants to remain in a “bubble” to prevent the spread of the virus.

2021-07-16.title: She’s One of China’s Biggest Stars. She’s Also Transgender.
2021-07-16.abstract: Jin Xing, the first person in China to openly undergo transition surgery, is a household name. But she says she’s no standard-bearer for the L.G.B.T.Q. community.

2021-07-16.title: High waters and washed-out roads are hampering the rescue efforts.
2021-07-16.abstract: Everywhere, rescue efforts during the flooding in Central Europe were hampered. Electricity and communications networks were down, roads and bridges were washed out, drinking water was scarce. The worst hit were thinly populated, rural areas.

2021-07-16.title: ‘It went so fast. You tried to do something and it was already too late.’
2021-07-16.abstract: Schule, a tiny village in Germany, was hard-hit by the flooding.

2021-07-17.title: The Extent of Flooding in the Hardest-Hit Areas of Europe
2021-07-17.abstract: A preliminary analysis of satellite imagery shows wide areas of flooding along rivers in western Germany and neighboring countries.

2021-07-17.title: The New Magazines and Journals Shaping Africa’s Literary Scene
2021-07-17.abstract: Lolwe, Doek and other digital publications are helping to amplify new voices from the continent.

2021-07-17.title: Back to Militias, the Chaotic Afghan Way of War
2021-07-17.abstract: Concerned by the Taliban’s offensive, regional power brokers are again recruiting and arming volunteer militias. But some fear the quick fix will lead to a wider breakdown.

2021-07-17.title: How One of the World’s Biggest Ships Jammed the Suez Canal
2021-07-17.abstract: Four months after the megaship Ever Given got stuck in the canal, neither the canal nor the shipping industry has addressed some of the most critical issues that led to the grounding.

2021-07-17.title: New evacuations are ordered in Germany, even as other residents return home after the floods.
2021-07-17.abstract: Even as some Germans managed to return to their homes on Saturday amid the receding floodwaters, the danger for others had not yet passed.

2021-07-18.title: U.S. Habit of Backing Strongman Allies Fed Turmoil in Haiti
2021-07-18.abstract: Washington dismissed warnings that democracy was unraveling under President Jovenel Moïse, leaving a gaping leadership void after his assassination.

2021-07-18.title: Boris Johnson Finds Himself in a Quandary Over Racism and Sports
2021-07-18.abstract: The British prime minister is under fire for failing to condemn crowds who booed when England’s soccer players took a knee during the European Championship.

2021-07-18.title: Boris Johnson to Self-Isolate, Reversing Plan to Enter N.H.S. Pilot Program
2021-07-18.abstract: The British prime minister and his top finance official were “pinged” by the health service’s test-and-trace app as contacts of the health minister, who has tested positive for the coronavirus.

2021-07-18.title: Merkel Visits Flood Region as Toll Continues to Mount
2021-07-18.abstract: The chancellor met with those who lost their homes and with rescue teams and German Army troops searching for survivors, as the number of dead rose above 180 in Europe.

2021-07-18.title: Australia’s Liz Cambage says the thought of the Olympics’ Covid bubble gave her panic attacks.
2021-07-18.abstract: The basketball star explained her decision to withdraw from the Games in a series of Instagram videos.

2021-07-19.title: This Train Crash Killed 49 People. It Didn’t Have to Happen.
2021-07-19.abstract: A Times investigation found that systemic failures at a government agency contributed to Taiwan’s worst rail disaster in seven decades.

2021-07-19.title: Surviving Australian Hotel Quarantine
2021-07-19.abstract: Nothing prepares you for the purgatory of a hotel room that could be anywhere in the world.

2021-07-19.title: ‘Anti-Sex’ Beds in the Olympic Village? A Social Media Theory Is Soon Debunked
2021-07-19.abstract: The coronavirus has forced a number of social distancing measures at the Summer Games, but the recyclable cardboard beds provided by organizers are not one of them.

2021-07-19.title: In France, angry protests, rising infections and record vaccinations.
2021-07-19.abstract: More than 100,000 people took to the streets over President Emmanuel Macron’s plan for a restrictive “health pass.” But far more booked to be vaccinated.

2021-07-19.title: The beds at the Tokyo Olympics are cardboard. Counter to rumors, that’s not a Covid precaution against sex.
2021-07-19.abstract: Some have shared images on social media of the modular bed frames

2021-07-20.title: Pedro Castillo, Leftist Political Outsider, Wins Peru Presidency
2021-07-20.abstract: Mr. Castillo, who has vowed to overhaul the political and economic system to address poverty and inequality, narrowly defeated the daughter of a jailed former president.

2021-07-20.title: 2 Rockets Fired at Israel From Lebanon, Setting Off Sirens in North
2021-07-20.abstract: One of the rockets was shot down by missile defenses and the other landed in open area, causing no damage, Israel’s military said.

2021-07-20.title: Are You in an Interfaith Relationship in India?
2021-07-20.abstract: Religious diversity has defined India for centuries, but interfaith unions remain rare. The Times wants to know the social and political forces at work to separate or support those relationships.

2021-07-20.title: Constant but Camouflaged, Flurry of Cyberattacks Offers Glimpse of New Era
2021-07-20.abstract: Once imagined as a new kind of warfare, government-linked hacking has instead become a widespread and perhaps permanent feature of the global order.

2021-07-20.title: She Said She Married for Love. Her Parents Called It Coercion.
2021-07-20.abstract: After a woman, who was born a Sikh, married a Muslim man, her parents accused him of kidnapping. Now, new laws across India are seeking to ban all interfaith marriages.

2021-07-21.title: 1946: British Bakeries Sell Out Before Bread Rationing Begins
2021-07-21.abstract: Britain rationed many foodstuffs during World War II, but extended rationing to bread only in the lean years immediately after. Bread remained rationed until 1948, and some food rationing continued until 1954.

2021-07-21.title: Canadian province declares a state of emergency, evacuating thousands.
2021-07-21.abstract: Nearly 300 fires in British Columbia have prompted evacuation orders affecting more than 5,700 people, officials said.

2021-07-21.title: A top political prisoner dies of Covid in Myanmar, with others in critical condition.
2021-07-21.abstract: The death of U Nyan Win, a lawyer for Aung San Suu Kyi who was locked up for months after a February coup, pointed to the growing toll of Myanmar’s outbreak.

2021-07-21.title: Liverpool Loses Its UNESCO World Heritage Status
2021-07-21.abstract: A committee voted to strip the city in northwest England of its status because of concerns about redevelopment, most notably on its waterfront.

2021-07-21.title: Kodak Deletes Post by Photographer Who Called Xinjiang an ‘Orwellian Dystopia’
2021-07-21.abstract: After public backlash in China, the American company dropped an Instagram post featuring images of a region where accusations of human rights violations have drawn intense scrutiny.

2021-07-22.title: A former Boris Johnson adviser makes new accusations about the U.K. prime minister’s Covid response.
2021-07-22.abstract: The former aide said that Mr. Johnson expressed hesitancy about ordering a second shutdown last fall, saying that “the people who are dying are essentially all over 80.”

2021-07-22.title: More than 1.5 million children have lost a caregiver to the pandemic, a study says.
2021-07-22.abstract: The number of children in need is only expected to grow as Covid-19 cases rise in areas short of vaccines and as variants spread.

2021-07-22.title: Japan Coronavirus Map and Case Count
2021-07-22.abstract: See the latest charts and maps of coronavirus cases, deaths, hospitalizations and vaccinations in Japan.

2021-07-22.title: The 1964 Games Proclaimed a New Japan. There’s Less to Cheer This Time.
2021-07-22.abstract: The country has changed vastly from that hopeful moment nearly six decades ago, and the Olympics this time around have come to represent something different and not entirely positive.

2021-07-22.title: Singapore wanted to ‘live normally’ with Covid. An outbreak has brought back restrictions.
2021-07-22.abstract: Singapore is one of several countries in Asia, Europe and the Americas that have been encouraging people to mostly return to their daily rhythms and transition to a kind of new pandemic normal. Now it faces hard choices about how to handle the rising caseloads that reopenings tend to bring.

2021-07-23.title: The Delta Variant Taught Me to Waste Time. And I’m Grateful.
2021-07-23.abstract: Searching for serenity during another stubborn lockdown, I’m learning to let my mind wander, far away from the news.

2021-07-23.title: Tense Funeral for Haiti Leader Exposes Rifts, and U.S. Delegation Departs Early
2021-07-23.abstract: Angry protests before and during the funeral reflected the nation’s deep divisions, a backdrop to a moment many had hoped would help mend a fractured nation. Shots were reported outside the event.

2021-07-23.title: How a Frontline Nurse Trained for the Olympics in a Time of Pandemic
2021-07-23.abstract: When the coronavirus hit her home country, Joan Poh, Singapore’s only female rower competing in Tokyo, had to juggle a return to her hospital work with getting ready for the Games.

2021-07-23.title: The bubble bursts: New Zealand suspends quarantine-free travel from Australia.
2021-07-23.abstract: The “travel bubble” is paused for at least eight weeks amid rising Covid cases in Australia, where an outbreak in New South Wales has been declared a national emergency.

2021-07-23.title: They resist being vaccinated despite living in one of New York City’s virus hot spots.
2021-07-23.abstract: Staten Island’s recent uptick in coronavirus cases was a harbinger of a larger increase across New York City.

2021-07-24.title: After a Republican outcry, Tennessee redirects vaccine outreach to teenagers, shifting it to their parents.
2021-07-24.abstract: Two weeks ago, a campaign to encourage public school students to get vaccinated was halted when Republican elected officials complained that parents should make the decisions.

2021-07-24.title: Japan’s Diverse Olympic Stars Reflect a Country That’s Changing (Slowly)
2021-07-24.abstract: Multiracial athletes like Naomi Osaka and Rui Hachimura are helping to redefine what it means to be Japanese. But they are often still seen as outsiders.

2021-07-24.title: A Rebellion Is Stirring in Boris Johnson’s Backyard
2021-07-24.abstract: Brexit helped destroy Labour’s “red wall” in England’s North. Now, it’s coming for the Conservatives’ “blue wall” in the South.

2021-07-24.title: Spain Pledged Citizenship to Sephardic Jews. Now They Feel Betrayed.
2021-07-24.abstract: In 2015, Spain said it would give citizenship to the descendants of Sephardic Jews expelled during the Spanish Inquisition. Then rejections started pouring in this summer.

2021-07-24.title: They Were Once Luxury Venues. Now They Are Grim Covid Camps.
2021-07-24.abstract: As an outbreak seizes Cambodia, patients who test positive for the virus say they are being forced into quarantine centers that are more like makeshift prisons than hospitals.

2021-07-25.title: The Fisherwomen, Chevron and the Leaking Pipe
2021-07-25.abstract: Big Oil has a filthy legacy in the Niger Delta region of Nigeria. One prominent environmentalist said: “They are moving out and leaving all the mess behind.”

2021-07-25.title: In Nagorno-Karabakh, Land Mines, Bulldozers and Lingering Tensions
2021-07-25.abstract: Despite the hurdles, territory seized by Azerbaijan from Armenia in last year’s war is being transformed with breathtaking speed.

2021-07-25.title: Disinformation for Hire, a Shadow Industry, Is Quietly Booming
2021-07-25.abstract: Back-alley firms meddle in elections and promote falsehoods on behalf of clients who can claim deniability, escalating our era of unreality.

2021-07-25.title: No Longer ‘Hidden Victims,’ Children Are Dying as Virus Surges in Indonesia
2021-07-25.abstract: Hundreds have died from Covid-19 in recent weeks, many of them under the age of 5. National leaders face mounting criticism that they have been unprepared.

2021-07-25.title: Landslide Kills 9 in Northern India and Destroys Bridge
2021-07-25.abstract: Dramatic video showed boulders rolling down into a valley in Himachal Pradesh state in the Himalayas.

2021-07-26.title: Biden’s China Strategy Meets Resistance at the Negotiating Table
2021-07-26.abstract: Washington hopes to find areas of collaboration, while also confronting Beijing on disputed issues. But talks between the two sides began with harsh words from Chinese officials.

2021-07-26.title: Ending Bitter 3-Month Standoff, Samoa’s Leader Concedes Election Defeat
2021-07-26.abstract: Fiame Naomi Mata’afa is scheduled to take office as the Pacific Island nation’s first female prime minister.

2021-07-26.title: As China Boomed, It Didn’t Take Climate Change Into Account. Now It Must.
2021-07-26.abstract: China’s breathtaking economic growth created cities ill-equipped to face extreme weather. Last week’s dramatic floods showed that much will have to change.

2021-07-26.title: Tunisia’s Democracy Verges on Collapse as President Moves to Take Control
2021-07-26.abstract: The effort by President Kais Saied threatened the only democracy to emerge from the Arab Spring protests a decade ago, and his opponents called it a coup.

2021-07-26.title: As China boomed, it didn’t take climate change into account. Now it must.
2021-07-26.abstract: China’s breakneck growth over the last four decades erected soaring cities where there had been hamlets and farmland. The cities lured factories, and the factories lured workers. The boom lifted hundreds of millions of people out of the poverty and rural hardship they once faced.

2021-07-27.title: North and South Korea Reopen Communication Hotlines After a Tense 14 Months
2021-07-27.abstract: Restoring the hotlines signaled a thaw in inter-Korean relations, which chilled after the collapse of Kim Jong-un’s diplomacy with former President Donald J. Trump.

2021-07-27.title: Hong Kong Protester Is Convicted in First Trial Under Tough Security Law
2021-07-27.abstract: Tong Ying-kit, 24, who struck police officers with his motorcycle while carrying a banner, could be sentenced to life in prison.

2021-07-27.title: Lockdowns ease in parts of Australia, but Sydney’s outbreak swells.
2021-07-27.abstract: Two Australian states will come out of lockdown on Wednesday night, as the authorities believe they have suppressed clusters of the infectious Delta variant. But in Sydney, the country’s biggest city, an outbreak that has swelled to 2,000 cases shows no signs of abating.

2021-07-27.title: ‘They Have My Sister’: As Uyghurs Speak Out, China Targets Their Families
2021-07-27.abstract: Activists overseas who denounce China’s repression in Xinjiang have found that relatives back home were imprisoned, or worse.

2021-07-27.title: As virus cases rise, another contagion spreads among the vaccinated: anger.
2021-07-27.abstract: As coronavirus cases surge again across the United States, many inoculated people are losing patience with vaccine holdouts who they say are neglecting a civic duty or clinging to misinformation even as new patients arrive in emergency rooms and the nation renews mask advisories.

2021-07-28.title: Want a Vaccine Reservation in South Korea? Try Waiting 111 Hours.
2021-07-28.abstract: Once a model in fighting the pandemic, the country has been slow to vaccinate its people, even as it is being hit by its worst-ever wave of infections.

2021-07-28.title: South Korea’s vaccine campaign stumbles as infections rise.
2021-07-28.abstract: When South Koreans logged on to a government website this month to book Covid-19 vaccine appointments, a pop-up window told them there was “just a bit” of a delay.

2021-07-28.title: They Were Driven From Their Homes. Then Floods Struck Their Camps.
2021-07-28.abstract: Heavy rains and landslides in Bangladesh have killed at least 11 Rohingya refugees and destroyed entire hillsides of shelters.

2021-07-28.title: China Offers the Taliban a Warm Welcome While Urging Peace Talks
2021-07-28.abstract: Worried about the war in Afghanistan, China has stepped up diplomatic efforts with the government and the group to encourage a political settlement after the U.S. withdrawal.

2021-07-28.title: Poles Tussle Over an Icon of Their Past, With an Eye on the Future
2021-07-28.abstract: A tug of war over the legacy of the Solidarity movement has much to do with the battle over which direction the country should go today.

2021-07-29.title: Nationals postpone M.L.B. game after 4 players and 8 staff members test positive.
2021-07-29.abstract: The infections came a day after the Nationals’ shortstop Trea Turner left during Tuesday night’s game against the Phillies after testing positive.

2021-07-29.title: The F.D.A. extends the Johnson & Johnson vaccine’s shelf life to six months.
2021-07-29.abstract: The decision comes after state health officials were worried that doses of the company’s coronavirus shots would expire and go to waste.

2021-07-29.title: The Chinese Sports Machine’s Single Goal: The Most Golds, at Any Cost
2021-07-29.abstract: China relies on a system that puts tens of thousands of children in government-run training schools. Many of the young athletes are funneled into less prominent sports that Beijing hopes to dominate.

2021-07-29.title: This Ethiopian Road Is a Lifeline for Millions. Now It’s Blocked.
2021-07-29.abstract: Aid workers say the Ethiopian government has effectively cut off the lone route into the conflict-torn region of Tigray, leading to a risk of mass starvation.

2021-07-29.title: Thailand builds a Covid treatment center at an international airport.
2021-07-29.abstract: Officials in Thailand on Wednesday began building a Covid-19 treatment center on the grounds of an international airport as the country grapples with a prolonged surge in infections.

2021-07-30.title: Don’t Let the Pandemic Tear Australia Apart
2021-07-30.abstract: Our states sometimes seem to be at war with each other as outbreaks flare. But this virus knows no boundaries.

2021-07-30.title: Hong Kong Protester Is Sentenced to 9 Years in First Security Law Case
2021-07-30.abstract: Tong Ying-kit, 24, who drove a motorcycle into police officers while carrying a protest flag, was convicted of terrorism and inciting secession.

2021-07-30.title: The Indigenous Archaeologist Tracking Down the Missing Residential Children
2021-07-30.abstract: Kisha Supernant has brought radar technology to the search for burial sites in Canada while she works to reshape her profession’s relationship with Indigenous communities.

2021-07-30.title: An outbreak of the Delta variant tests China’s zero-tolerance approach to the virus.
2021-07-30.abstract: Cases have spread from the city of Nanjing to several provinces and the capital, Beijing, in the past three weeks.

2021-07-30.title: ‘They Thought I Was Dead’: Haitian President’s Widow Recounts Assassination
2021-07-30.abstract: Struck by gunfire, Martine Moïse lay bleeding as the assassins who killed her husband ransacked her room. Now, she says, the F.B.I. must find the mastermind behind the attack.

2021-07-31.title: ‘It Feels Like We’re Just Waiting to Die’: Hong Kong Targets Student Unions
2021-07-31.abstract: The authorities are clamping down on universities, which they consider hotbeds for unrest. The groups say they are fighting for survival.

2021-07-31.title: Body of Reuters Photographer Was Mutilated in Taliban Custody, Officials Say
2021-07-31.abstract: The body of Danish Siddiqui, a Pulitzer Prize winner killed in Afghanistan, was unrecognizable when it was brought to a Kandahar hospital, one official said.

2021-07-31.title: In South Africa, Poachers Now Traffic in Tiny Succulent Plants
2021-07-31.abstract: Police search the desert trying to track down the poachers selling Conophytums to collectors worldwide, threatening to wipe out rare plants in the wild.

2021-07-31.title: As Fears Grip Afghanistan, Hundreds of Thousands Flee
2021-07-31.abstract: With the Taliban sweeping across much of the country, at least 30,000 Afghans are leaving each week. Many more have been displaced within Afghanistan’s borders.

2021-07-31.title: The Long Kiss Goodbye: Will Covid End the French Bise Forever?
2021-07-31.abstract: Social distancing made the cheek kiss a much rarer greeting, and polling suggests it may stay that way. Some say good riddance, but others miss the warmth of times past.
